spec_match.rationale: Uses the `paintsubtree` event alongside canvas `layoutSubtree = true` to coordinate dynamic repaint loops directly from DOM invalidations.

interactive.rationale: Provides start/stop controls for a dynamic dashboard ticker, manual ping buttons, tick-rate sliders, and a live scrollable event log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Directly highlights the paintsubtree event loop mechanics, ensuring developers see how the invalidation pipeline is kept live.

edge_cases.rationale: Features a robust 30Hz polling fallback for browsers lacking the paint-subtree API, ensuring the interactive simulation remains informative.

references.rationale: Appropriately links to ChromeStatus feature 5172548013916160 as well as the WHATWG specification pull request.

design_system.rationale: Layout fits the design system cleanly, with clear contrast, readable logs, and appropriate spacing.

openQuestions.title: Clarify hardware acceleration or frame boundary delays
openQuestions.detail: The paint event rate reflects the tick rate capped at VSync. It would be helpful to explain if layoutSubtree triggers a new paint event even when the invalidation has no visual impact (e.g., replacing text with identical characters). Add a small note/tooltip in the explanation.

summary: An outstanding concept page that perfectly models the paintsubtree event hook, with excellent visual diagnostics, interactive rate toggles, robust fallback mechanics, and flawless specification references.
